title: Crispy Thai Chicken Satay
description: Umami-packed, anti-inflammatory Thai satay with a miso-marinated chicken breast and a silky peanut sauce.
instructions: Trim and slice chicken breasts into 2-inch strips.
In a bowl whisk together miso paste, coconut milk, lime juice, ginger, garlic, turmeric, cumin, coriander, chili flakes, honey, soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Toss chicken strips in the miso mixture, cover, and refrigerate for 20 minutes.
Soak bamboo skewers in water for 30 minutes while chicken marinates.
Preheat a grill or grill pan over medium-high heat.
Thread marinated chicken onto skewers, leaving a little space between pieces.
Grill the satay for 3-4 minutes per side, or until the chicken is cooked through and slightly charred.
While grilling whisk the peanut sauce: combine peanut butter, fish sauce, lime juice, honey, and a splash of water until smooth and pourable.
Serve the hot satay skewers with the peanut sauce on the side and a sprinkle of fresh cilantro if desired.
